Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Dec-02-2015 Advanced From Dec-29-15 To Dec-15-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Dec-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-14-297718 The Susan Lee Trust, PTR-14-297719 The Stephen Lee Irrevocable Trust, PTR-15-299315 The Stephen Lee & Susan Lee Irrevocable Trust, PTR-15-299316 The Amended and Restated Stephen & Susan Lee Trust.
Appearances: David Brillant & Daniel Rottinghaus for (petitioner) Sharon Seto. David Baer for Eva Wong, Peter Lee & Anne Lee. Robert Riggs for the other Lees.

The Court expressed its happiness that this is now on for a petition to approve settlement agreement, stated that the procedural issues have been cleared and confirmed that there is nothing additional to report. The Court granted the petitions to approve settlement agreements (in all cases) (subject to review of the order), stated that the other petitions will be taken off calendar and advised counsel that it will not sign the whole settlement, only the order. Mr. Baer asked about the timeline on the sales orders and was told not long.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts . (Not Reported)
Ex Parte Application For Order Shortening Time To Advance Hearing Date On Petition To Approve Settlement

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account And Provide Requested Information
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Continued To Aug-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-14-297718 The Susan Lee Trust and PTR-14-297719 The Stephen K. Lee Trust.

Appearances: Daniel Rottinghaus & David Brillant for (trustee) Sharon Seto. Michael Gerson for (petitioners) Peter Lee, Anne Lee and Ava Wong.

The Court stated that some discovery has been done and asked if there are still issues to set for trial. Mr. Rottinghaus stated that Ms. Seto is a client in other actions, confirmed that he has substituted in for Mary Mayo on both cases and reported that Mr. Brillant has associated in as he has expertise in Probate. Mr. Gerson stated that he did not know until today that Mr. Rottinghaus and Mr. Brillant had substituted in on both cases, complained about the many continuances, now new counsel and that the matter keeps dragging out. The court stated that another reality is that there may not be information to get as much of an accounting as you are entitled to and that with new counsel it makes sense to continue the matter for everyone to talk and decide if the case needs to go to trial. Mr. Brillant stated that they would need 60 days to do the research and to respond to the examiner's notes. The court stated that it would be a tremendous value if they are able to do the accounting and get this done. The Petitions to compel trustee to account and provide requested information and the accounting petitions (in both cases) were continued to August 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. Court Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott, CSR #12552, 877-451-1580 (Reported)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account And Provide Requested Information
Off Calendar Mar-03-2015 Continued To May-12-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard: PTR-14-297718 Susan Lee Trust and PTR-14-297719 Stephen K. Lee trust.
Appearances: David Baer & Sung Shim for (petitioners) Anne Lee, Eva Wong & Peter Lee. Mary Mayo for (trustee) Sharon Seto.

The court listed the various petitions and motions currently set in the two cases. Mr. Baer stated that everything should be continued to May 4, 2015, at that time consider where they are, felt it would be wasteful to have an evidentiary hearing, and stated the question now is are the objections valid or should the accountings be approved. Ms. Mayo stated she needed guidance on the accounting. The court stated it had no opinion today, objections have been recently filed which the court has not been able to review and stated that Ms. Mayo could address the objections through supplements or responses. The petition to compel trustee to account, etc., and the accounting petition in the Susan Lee Trust, the petition to compel trustee to account, etc., and the accounting petition currently set for May 4, 2015 in the Stephen Lee Trust are continued to May 12, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. Court Reporter Jennifer Seelenbacher, CSR #11878, 877-451-1580 (Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-03-2015 Continued To May-12-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account And Provide Requested Information
Off Calendar Jan-20-2015 Continued To Mar-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard together: PTR-14-297718 The Susan Lee Trust & PTR-14-297719 The Stepehen K. Lee Irrevocable Trust.
Appearances: David Baer & Robert Riggs for (petitioners) Eva Wong, Peter Lee & Anne Lee. Mary Mayo for (trustee) Sharon Seto.

The court stated that we are here for the status of the petitions and that there has been some discovery. Ms. Mayo provided a summary indicating that the Susan Lee trust received the proceeds of the life insurance policies in 2005, all funds were distributed, no objections to distribution were made, an accounting is now being requested and they have provided all of the information they have. Ms. Mayo also stated that the Stephen Lee trust is ongoing, the only activity is the payment of insurance premiums (AXA & MetLife), Ms. Seto took over in 2005 with the help of Peter Lee, has been able to obtain statements back to that date and expects to have the accounting petition for 12/2005 - 12/31/14 filed this week. Ms. Mayo reported that Peter Lee stopped making payments to the trust in May, is concerned that the two insurance policies will be cancelled due to lack of payment of premiums unless the court orders all parties to share payment. Mr. Baer stated that Ms. Seto has been the trustee from 1987, the accounting needs to go back to that date, there is no waiver of accountings, is confused as to who owns or who the beneficiaries are of the insurance policies. Ms. Mayo stated there are no funds to hire a forensic accountant, there are no funds to do anything, they will file the accounting petition based on what they have. The matters in both cases were continued to March 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of current petitions. If the accounting petitions are filed with proper notice to be heard on March 3, 2015 it will be authorized.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. Court Reporter Holly Sayers, CSR #13678, 877-451-1580.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account And Provide Requested Information
Off Calendar Nov-18-2014 Continued To Jan-20-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-14-297718 The Susan Lee Trust and PTR-14-297719 The Stephen K. Lee Irrevocable Trust.

Appearances: Robert Riggs for Peter Lee (Petitioner) also has authority to represent Anne Lee and Eva Wong. Mary Mayo for Sharon Seto (Respondent/Trustee).

Mr. Riggs stated that mediation did not resolve the issues, that other Counsel is associating in for all petitioners and that they are requesting a trial date in March. Ms. Mayo stated that the matter is not at issue and not ready for trial, that discovery has not been propounded, that there is another civil action and that she would like to see them go to trial first. Mr. Riggs stated that he wants an accounting in both Trusts and that he is not sure if he will file a petition for approval or they will be informal accountings. Counsel also spoke of how far back they the accountings should go and the various policies that were in the Trusts. Both Petitions to Compel Trustee to Account, etc. were continued to January 20, 2015 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Early Langley, CSR#3537, (877) 451-1580. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Reported)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account And Provide Requested Information
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Continued To Nov-18-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-14-297718 The Susan Lee Trust and PTR-14-297719 The Stephen K. Lee Irrevocable Trust.

Appearances: Evan Nadel for Eva Wong (Petitioner). Mary Mayo for Sharon Seto (Trustee). Robert Riggs for Peter Lee. Robert DeVries for Anne Lee.

Mr. Riggs requested a 60-day continuance stating that some discovery responses were received and that they have mediation scheduled on Monday with JAMS. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Account, etc. was continued to November 18, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on trial setting. Counsel can contact the Probate Examiner if more time is needed.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account And Provide Requested Information
Off Calendar Aug-19-2014 Continued To Sep-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-14-297718 The Susan Lee Trust and PTR-14-297719 The Stephen K. Lee Irrevocable Trust.

Appearances: Kenneth Katzoff and Robert Riggs for Peter Lee. Evan Nadel for Eva Wong. Robert DeVries. Mary Mayo for and with Sharon Seto (Respondent).

The Parties stated their positions with regard to information not being provided for the 6 or 7 policies, circumstances of the purchase of said policies, whether they were sold, policy numbers not being provided, whether they were part of the Trust, missing files, the proceeds that were received from Susan Lee's insurance policy, etc. Both Petitions to Compel Trustee to Account and Provide Information were continued to September 23, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Parties to advise the Court as to 1) whether they had informal discovery on the 6 or 7 policies for $58,000,000.00 2) readiness for trial in case number 297719 and 3) status on discovery.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Angela Sinclair, CSR# 13902, (877)451-1580. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chin. (Reported)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account And Provide Requested Information
Off Calendar Jun-30-2014 Continued To Aug-19-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account And Provide Requested Information
Off Calendar Jun-10-2014 Continued To Jul-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-14-297718 The Susan Lee Trust and PTR-14-297719 In Re Stephen K. Lee Irrevocable Trust

Appearances: Kenneth Katzoff and Nathan Coppernoll for and with Peter Lee. Mary Mayo for Sharon Lee Seto (Trustee/Respondent). Evan Nadel for Eva Wong (Petitioner). Robert De Vries for Anne Lee.

Discussion was held with regard to six other partition actions in San Francisco between the Siblings, lack of information from the Trustee, no annual accounting or final accounting being provided in Susan's Trust, documents not being produced, transfer of Trust funds for a $1,000,000.00 loan to a Cousin and $1,000,000.00 to an off shore account in British Virgin Island (Seto Investments), sales of life insurance policies, how discovery is to be conducted, the possibility of mediation, a ninth matter involving a San Jose property, etc. The Court suggested/requested that the Parties do informal exchange of information as much as possible. The Court will send all nine matters to a settlement conference with the Court's Settlement Officer to be scheduled in August. Counsel is to provide the Clerk with a list of all nine case numbers. Both Petitions to Compel Trustee to Account were continued to July 15, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
